name: read_file_tool
summary: Read the contents of a file, optionally returning a specific range of lines.
Returns text with line numbers prefixed so the model can cite `file:line` and target follow-up edits precisely.
constraints: Each output line is `<line_no>\t<line>`, starting at 1.
For large files, pass `offset` and `limit` to read only the relevant slice rather than the whole file.
The path is resolved relative to the agent's working directory.
anti_patterns: To find files by name — use `glob_tool`.
To search across many files — use `grep_tool`.
cautions: ALWAYS read the file before proposing an edit to it. The edit tool will refuse otherwise.
output: Plain text. One line per file line, formatted `<line_no>\t<line>`.
Line numbers are absolute (they reflect the file's own numbering, not the slice). Empty string for an empty file or a slice past EOF.

path.description: Path to the file to read.

offset.description: 1-based line number to start reading from (default: 1).

limit.description: Number of lines to read (default: to end of file). Combine with `offset` to read a slice.
